title: Fix CSV/Excel parser to extract gateway details and packet stats from uploaded file format

requirements.text: Update the CSV/Excel parser in csvParser.ts to correctly detect and parse the file format shown in the uploaded screenshots. The file has a metadata header block in rows 1–7 before the data header row (row 8). The parser must stop throwing 'No data rows found' and instead correctly identify row 8 as the header row (blue highlighted row) and rows 9+ as data rows.

quotes: No data rows found in the uploaded files. Please check the file contents.
Uploading file will be in this format

acceptanceCriteria: Uploading the Excel file no longer shows 'No data rows found' error
Parser correctly skips metadata rows 1–7 (Customer Name, Group, Gateway, Start Date, End Date) and identifies row 8 as the column header row
Data rows starting from row 9 are successfully parsed

requirements.text: Extract gateway details from the metadata rows (rows 1–6) of the uploaded file and use them to auto-populate form fields or report data. Specifically: parse the Gateway name (row 4, e.g. 'Gateway : N13-5_D20_Lite') as the unit name, parse the Start Date from row 5 ('Start Date & Time : 8 Feb'26 12:00 AM'), and parse the End Date / current date from row 6 ('End Date & Time : 23 Feb'26 12:00 AM').

quotes: From the file you can get the gateway details like unit name, start date and current date

acceptanceCriteria: Unit name is extracted from the 'Gateway :' metadata row and populated in the import form or report entry
Start date is extracted from the 'Start Date & Time :' metadata row
End date (current date) is extracted from the 'End Date & Time :' metadata row
Extracted values are shown to the user before submission (e.g. in the parsed preview)

requirements.text: Update the parser to compute total packet count and stored packet count from the data rows. The column header 'PktState' is located in spreadsheet column Y (25th column, zero-indexed column 24). Count all data rows where PktState is 'Normal' for total packets, and count rows where PktState is 'Stored' for stored packet count. Map these counts to the totalPackets and storedPackets fields of the ParsedRow output.

quotes: For Total packets and stored packet count you can fetch the data from 'PktState' in 'Y' column Normal & Stored had been mentioned.

acceptanceCriteria: Parser finds the 'PktState' column by header name (not hardcoded index) in the header row
Rows with PktState value 'Normal' are counted as total/normal packets
Rows with PktState value 'Stored' are counted as stored packets
Both counts are correctly reflected in the imported report entry

requirements.text: Update the parser to compute GPS valid fix count from the data rows. The column header 'GPS Status' is located in spreadsheet column O (15th column, zero-indexed column 14). Count all data rows where GPS Status is 'Valid' (case-insensitive) as GPS valid fix packets. Map this count to the validGpsPackets field of the ParsedRow output.

quotes: For GPS valid fix can be fetched from 'GPS Status' from column 'O'

acceptanceCriteria: Parser finds the 'GPS Status' column by header name in the header row
Rows where GPS Status equals 'Valid' (case-insensitive) are counted as GPS valid fix packets
Rows where GPS Status is 'Invalid' or other values are not counted as valid GPS fixes
The valid GPS fix count is correctly reflected in the imported report entry

requirements.text: After successfully parsing the file, display a summary preview to the user showing the extracted gateway details (unit name, start date, end date) and computed statistics (total packet count, stored packet count, GPS valid fix count) before the user submits the import. This replaces or augments the existing per-file parsed row preview in CSVImportSection.tsx.

quotes: Uploading file will be in this format and i had attached the error in dashboard tool and the screenshot of the file i had opened and sent

acceptanceCriteria: After a successful file parse, a preview card is shown with unit name, start date, end date, total packets, stored packets, and GPS valid fix count
The preview appears before the user clicks submit/import
If parsing fails or any key field is missing, a clear error message is shown instead

constraints: Column lookups for 'PktState' and 'GPS Status' must use header name matching, not hardcoded column index, to be robust to column reordering
Metadata row parsing must handle the 'Key : Value' text format (e.g. 'Gateway : N13-5_D20_Lite')
Do not break existing CSV/TSV parsing functionality
nonGoals: Changes to the backend data model or Motoko actor
Support for file formats other than CSV, TSV, XLS, XLSX
Automatic GPS coordinate parsing or map display
